_comment: PROTOTYPE for schema v2.0.0 (ticket #60). Real public facts, assembled by hand to make the schema fall out of something read — not a live pipeline run. Read-throughs and interpretation are illustrative. Facts are grounded in docs/research/program-detective-source-set-2026.md and its cited primaries.

program.name: HMBD-001
program.one_line: A HER3 signalling antibody differentiated from the HER3 ADC field — it blocks the heregulin-driven signalling node rather than delivering a payload to HER3-expressing cells.
program.clinical_stage: Phase 1b in squamous NSCLC (chemotherapy backbone, ± cetuximab)

headline.title: ESMO 2026 titles land with HER3 and squamous readouts in HMBD-001's lane — text still embargoed
headline.summary: ESMO 2026 regular-abstract titles posted 17 Jul, one cycle after the arena's defining event: ivonescimab's HARMONi-6 first-line squamous-NSCLC overall-survival win (HR 0.66) at ASCO 2026 in June. No mechanism twin — a HER3 antibody blocking the same signalling node — has surfaced in the clinic; every moving HER3 competitor is an ADC or bispecific ADC, a target twin, not a mechanism twin.
headline.so_what: The bar HMBD-001's Phase 1b must eventually clear is being reset in first line by an ADC-and-bispecific field, while HMBD-001's actual mechanistic differentiation faces no direct rival. That is simultaneously the opportunity and the exposure: the signalling thesis is unproven precisely because no one else is testing it.

tldr_bullets.text: ESMO 2026 titles dropped 17 Jul; a HER3-DXd squamous cohort title appeared, text embargoed to the autumn congress.
Ivonescimab's HARMONi-6 OS win (HR 0.66) resets the first-line squamous bar HMBD-001's later-line combo must eventually beat.
SDP0505 (HER3 x c-Met bispecific ADC) remains a China-first blind spot — CDE/chictr, no clean free feed; China BLA still targeted 2026.
Still no mechanism twin in the clinic: HMBD-001's signalling-blockade MOA has no direct HER3-antibody rival.

items.catalyst: China BLA submission for SDP0505 in EGFR-TKI-resistant NSCLC
items.what_it_would_prove: That a HER3-directed ADC can reach registration in NSCLC ahead of any signalling-antibody data — target validation running well ahead of mechanism validation.

items.catalyst: ESMO 2026 presentation of the HER3-DXd cohort whose title posted 17 Jul
items.what_it_would_prove: Whether the DXd ADC is being steered toward the squamous setting HMBD-001's Phase 1b occupies — direct arena encroachment vs a non-overlapping cohort.

competitors.name: Patritumab deruxtecan (HER3-DXd)
competitors.summary: HER3-directed antibody-drug conjugate. HERTHENA-Lung02's OS miss led Daiichi Sankyo and Merck to voluntarily withdraw the EGFR-mutant-NSCLC BLA in May 2025; the program continues across ~15 tumour types. An ESMO 2026 title referencing a squamous cohort posted 17 Jul (text embargoed).

read_through.text: Same target (HER3), different MOA (an ADC delivering a payload, not blocking signalling). A HER3-DXd win validates HER3 as expressed and druggable — it does not validate HMBD-001's signalling-blockade thesis, and an ADC failure does not refute it. The live risk is arena, not biology: if the ESMO cohort is squamous, an ADC readout would reset HMBD-001's Phase 1b setting before the signalling data exists to argue against it.

failure.note: Indication-tier failure: demoted and archived for EGFR-NSCLC while the program-tier entity survives across ~15 other tumour types. A target twin's failure is two-tier; only the affected indication archives.

competitors.name: SDP0505 (SHR9265)
competitors.summary: First-in-class HER3 x c-Met bispecific ADC, global Phase 2 in EGFR-TKI-resistant NSCLC, China BLA targeted 2026. Primary movement registers on China's CDE and chictr.org.cn, which have no clean free feed and are language-gated.

degradation.marker: China-first asset — registry movement tracked at low confidence via CDE/chictr; last verified from a journal review, not a live registry diff.

read_through.text: Another HER3 ADC (bispecific), so target-twin logic applies: it validates HER3 expression, not signalling blockade. Named here mainly because it is the competitor the detective is structurally worst at seeing — the China registry blind spot lands squarely on HMBD-001's closest modality-adjacent rival, so its read-through is deliberately hedged low-confidence rather than dropped.

indications.name: Squamous non-small cell lung cancer
indications.program_context: HMBD-001's Phase 1b setting: chemotherapy backbone ± cetuximab, later-line.

setting_rivals.name: Ivonescimab (AK112)
setting_rivals.summary: PD-1 x VEGF bispecific. HARMONi-6 showed an OS benefit (HR 0.66, 27.9 vs 23.7 months) over PD-1 plus chemotherapy in first-line squamous NSCLC, presented at the ASCO 2026 Plenary. Trial was China-only; regional generalizability is a live analyst dispute.

read_through.text: Not a HER3 asset — it shares HMBD-001's patients, not its biology. It resets the first-line squamous bar upward, which raises the efficacy any later-line HMBD-001 combo must eventually justify itself against, and compresses the window in which a differentiated mechanism can enter earlier lines. The China-only-trial caveat is the one lever that keeps the ex-China bar softer.
The floor HMBD-001's setting is measured against. It is the bar, not a mover: it changes the read-through only when it changes, which the ivonescimab OS win is now pressuring.

emerging.note: HARMONi-6 OS benefit; read-only view over the catalyst queue and setting-rival records.

lines.bar_direction: rising — ivonescimab's OS win is the first credible first-line displacement signal since KEYNOTE-407.
static — no primary-sourced later-line displacement this cycle; HMBD-001's own setting.

indications.name: NRG1-fusion solid tumours
indications.program_context: A priority indication where HMBD-001's signalling-blockade mechanism is most differentiated (NRG1 is the HER3 ligand), but with no active arena scan this cycle.

degradation.marker: Arena scan dormant this cycle — no-op landscape; last touched 2026-05-02. NRG1-fusion competition covered at the monthly knob, not per run.

critic_catches.claim: HER3-DXd 'refiled' its NSCLC BLA this month
critic_catches.detail: The only support was an aggregator repost of the May 2025 voluntary-withdrawal news re-dated to July 2026. No primary or trade source confirms a refiling; the withdrawal, not a refiling, is the established fact.

open_threads.thread: Whether the ESMO 2026 HER3-DXd cohort is squamous (arena overlap) or a non-overlapping tumour type
open_threads.next_expected: Abstract text at the ESMO 2026 congress, autumn 2026

dropped_with_receipt.name: A HER2 ADC squamous readout
dropped_with_receipt.dropped_because: off-target — HER2, not HER3; no typed relation to HMBD-001. Recorded so a future run does not rediscover it as novel.

newly_discovered.name: An anti-HER3 CAR-T candidate (preclinical)
newly_discovered.what_it_is: Preclinical anti-HER3 CAR-T disclosed in a conference poster; earliest-stage HER3 competitor surfaced this cycle.

read_through.text: Third distinct HER3 modality (after ADC and bispecific ADC) — reinforces the theme that HER3 is being attacked as an antigen, not as a signalling node, which is exactly HMBD-001's white space. Preclinical, so priority is low.

promotion_proposal.reason: Preclinical and modality-adjacent only; propose to track as a watching-tier interest, not a promoted competitor, until a trial registers.

proposes_interest.note: Emerging HER3 modalities beyond ADC (CAR-T, radioconjugate) — watch for the first that targets the signalling axis.

partnership_bd.name: Merck & Co. (MSD)
partnership_bd.summary: Owns HER3-DXd through its 2023 Daiichi Sankyo ADC license — the target twin above. Separately, cetuximab (marketed by Merck KGaA ex-US, a different corporate Merck) is the combination agent in HMBD-001's Phase 1b squamous arm.

read_through.text: The 'Merck' entanglement is two companies wearing one name: MSD is a rival that owns HMBD-001's closest target-twin ADC, while Merck KGaA's cetuximab sits inside HMBD-001's own trial. Read as BD signal, MSD's continued HER3 investment is a validation of the target that a partner-or-acquirer would price in; read as threat, it is the best-resourced HER3 rival in the field.

threat_financing.name: Daiichi Sankyo
threat_financing.summary: The DXd ADC platform is the modality engine behind HER3-DXd and a broad ADC franchise. Platform threat is a company-unit relation and lives here, not on the program's competitor list.

read_through.text: The threat to HMBD-001 is not any single DXd asset but the platform's ability to point a validated payload at whichever HER3 setting reads out first. A signalling antibody competes one indication at a time; a payload platform re-aims cheaply. This is the strategic case for HMBD-001 owning the mechanism white space rather than racing on target.

themes_and_signals.theme: HER3 is being validated as a target, not as a signalling node
themes_and_signals.argument: Every moving HER3 competitor this cycle attacks HER3 as an antigen to bind or deliver a payload to — ADC, bispecific ADC, CAR-T. None blocks the heregulin/NRG1 signalling axis, which is HMBD-001's mechanism. If this holds, HMBD-001's differentiation is real but also lonely: no comparator will de-risk the signalling thesis on its behalf.

proposes_interest.note: Track the first clinical HER3 asset that targets the signalling axis rather than expression — it would be HMBD-001's first true mechanism twin.

ranked.blind_spot: China-first HER3 and squamous assets (SDP0505 via CDE/chictr; Akeso/ivonescimab financing via HKEX 9926.HK)
ranked.why_it_matters: These are HMBD-001's closest modality-adjacent and setting rivals, and they are the ones the free-feed detective sees latest — language-gated registries and non-US-listed financing with no clean JSON feed.
ranked.mitigation: Journal/abstract review at the monthly knob; treat registry state as low-confidence between reviews.

ranked.why_it_matters: How the market prices a HER3 readout has no free substitute (Evaluate/broker reports are paywalled).
ranked.mitigation: Out of scope while the reader is HMBD-001's decision-owner, not an investor; parked, not bought.

thesis_updates.before: HER3 target validation and HER3 mechanism validation will advance together as the field matures.
thesis_updates.after: Target validation is running years ahead of mechanism validation: ADCs and bispecifics are proving HER3 is druggable while no clinical asset tests signalling blockade, leaving HMBD-001's thesis differentiated but uncorroborated.

advisory_findings.note: Read-through is deliberately hedged because the underlying registry state is China-gated; flagged so the hedge is visible, not silently thin.
chictr.org.cn poll returned no parseable delta this cycle; SDP0505 registry state carried forward at low confidence.
Not stale — ESMO 2026 title-drop verified 17 Jul; recorded to show the surge-arming path even when surge is not yet active.

diffs_observed.note: No in-window US-registry delta for tracked HER3-DXd NCTs this cycle; movement surfaced via ESMO title-drop instead.

registry_watch.coverage_note: Near-complete for US/global registry; partial for China-first assets (CDE/chictr), which is the rank-1 blind spot.

paywalled_flagged.claim: Analyst consensus on HER3-DXd's residual multi-tumour value post-withdrawal
paywalled_flagged.note: Primary paywalled — interpretation, not fact; out of scope for the decision-owner reader.

interests.note: HER3-DXd encroachment into the squamous setting — the arena-overlap risk.
Any clinical HER3 asset that targets the signalling axis (the first true mechanism twin).
China-first HER3 ADCs (SDP0505 and successors) despite the feed blind spot.
